data.value: The Avalanche are 37-9-9, leading the NHL in points, goal differential, and goals scored. Nathan MacKinnon at Hart Trophy level, Cale Makar the best defenseman on the planet.
Nathan MacKinnon didn't look 100% at the Winter Olympics, raising health concerns. Analyst warns this is a must-monitor situation heading into the playoffs.
Despite dealing with a long injury list, Tampa continues to roll. Kucherov on pace for 146 points, Vasilevskiy like a brick wall, and Tampa is 19-2 since December 20.
Carolina is running away with the Metropolitan Division for the fourth time in six seasons, allowing only 163 goals in 57 games. At +700, analysts call them the best value for those fading Colorado.
The Hurricanes lack a McDavid-level superstar finisher and have faced postseason scoring concerns for years. Questions remain about whether their system can carry them through four playoff rounds.
Minnesota surged since acquiring Quinn Hughes, going 16-5-5 in that stretch. Would have shorter odds if not in the same division as Colorado. A significant dark horse value play at +1900.
The back-to-back 2024-25 Stanley Cup champions are on the outside of the playoff picture looking in. Currently struggling to replicate their championship form despite historic dynasty pedigree.
Connor McDavid keeps Edmonton in contention, but the team's below-average defensive structure is a genuine liability heading into the playoffs.
Detroit is on the verge of their first playoff appearance since 2016, representing one of the better underdog stories in the league this year.
